tool.name: checktrans
tool.summary: Strictly compare frame-1 coding-sequence translations against expected protein records

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `checktrans` strictly translates DNA coding-sequence records in frame 1 under the standard genetic code and compares them against paired expected protein records. Epithema v1 keeps the pairing rule simple and deterministic: inputs must contain the same number of records and are compared by input order.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The interface accepts one nucleotide coding-sequence input and one protein input. Nucleotide records must translate cleanly in frame 1 with coding-sequence length divisible by three. Protein records may end with a single terminal `*`, and one terminal stop is normalized on both sides for comparison.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits a stable table report with one row per paired comparison. Report columns are `nucleotide_id`, `protein_id`, `matches`, `translated_terminal_stop`, `expected_terminal_stop`, and `detail`.

sections.title: Legacy Context
sections.content: This acceptance anchor keeps one historical-style `checktrans` invocation in view and compares the Epithema table payload against a committed expected output. The governed comparison validates strict frame-1 translation, terminal-stop normalization, and deterministic row ordering.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema checktrans`. Current validation covers the matching committed fixture pair, mismatch reporting, unequal record-count failure, and invalid-codon rejection.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not search alternative frames or infer record pairing by identifier. Inputs are compared in order only, and ambiguous or invalid coding input fails clearly rather than being tolerated.

artifacts.label: Translation-check nucleotide fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed coding-sequence fixture used for strict translation checking.

artifacts.label: Translation-check protein fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed expected protein fixture paired by input order with the committed coding sequences.

examples.title: Compare matching coding and protein records
examples.description: Strictly translates the committed coding-sequence fixture in frame 1 and compares the result against the paired committed protein fixture.

expected_outputs.label: Translation comparison table
expected_outputs.description: A stable row-per-pair report describing whether each strict frame-1 translation matched the expected protein record.
